prompt_rendered: What is the exact name and year of enactment of the main national housing policy (or equivalent program) currently active in Brazil? Provide a 1-3 sentence answer with the official name and year.
ground_truth: Programa Minha Casa, Minha Vida (MCMV), originally enacted by Lei nº 11.977 in 2009; suspended 2020-2023 and relaunched in 2023 by Lei nº 14.620/2023. Administered by Ministério das Cidades.

prompt_rendered: What was the most recent officially reported annual rate of forest loss (or the dominant environmental monitoring indicator) for Brazil in 2022-2024? Provide the value, unit, and source.
ground_truth: INPE PRODES 2023: 9,064 km² of deforestation in the Legal Amazon (Aug/2022 – Jul/2023); 2022 value was 11,594 km² (approximately 22% year-over-year reduction). PRODES is the official annual monitoring program.

prompt_rendered: Describe the institutional framework for environmental licensing of large infrastructure projects in Brazil in approximately 150-200 words. Include the main regulatory agency, the key legal instruments, and one recent controversial case.
ground_truth: Main agency: IBAMA (federal) plus state environmental agencies (OEMAs) based on project scope. Key instruments: Resolução CONAMA 001/1986 (EIA requirement), Resolução CONAMA 237/1997 (licensing competence), and the new general licensing law (PL 2.159/2021) partially approved 2024. Recent controversial cases: Ferrogrão railway (EF-170), Belo Monte hydroelectric revisions, and Equatorial Margin oil exploration (Foz do Amazonas).

prompt_rendered: Characterize patterns of income inequality in Brazil in approximately 150-200 words. Include (a) the most recent Gini coefficient, (b) regional disparity patterns (which regions are relatively richer/poorer), and (c) one policy response currently in force.
ground_truth: Gini 2022 (IBGE PNAD): 0.518, slight decline from 0.545 in 2018. Regional patterns: Nordeste is the most unequal and poorest macro-region; São Paulo state and the Sul region are less internally unequal and wealthier. Policy response: Bolsa Família was relaunched in 2023 with minimum R$ 600/family payments, alongside FGTS Saque-Aniversário and phased emergency aid programs.

prompt_rendered: List 5-8 of the most important government ministries, agencies, or regulatory bodies involved in climate change adaptation and mitigation policy in Brazil. For each, provide one sentence describing its primary role.
ground_truth: 1. Ministério do Meio Ambiente e Mudança do Clima (MMA) — federal climate policy coordination. 2. IBAMA — environmental licensing and enforcement. 3. ICMBio — protected areas management. 4. ANA (Agência Nacional de Águas) — water resources and climate adaptation. 5. Secretaria Nacional de Mudança do Clima (created 2023). 6. Casa Civil — cross-ministerial coordination of Plano Clima. 7. MAPA — adaptation in agricultural sectors. 8. Defesa Civil Nacional — disaster response.

prompt_rendered: List 5-8 of the most prominent non-governmental organizations (NGOs) or civil society organizations active in environmental conservation and climate advocacy in Brazil. For each, provide one sentence describing its primary focus.
ground_truth: 1. Instituto Socioambiental (ISA) — indigenous lands and Amazon policy. 2. WWF-Brasil — biodiversity conservation. 3. Greenpeace Brasil — advocacy and campaigns. 4. SOS Mata Atlântica — Atlantic Forest conservation. 5. Imazon — Amazon monitoring and policy research. 6. Conservation International Brazil — protected area support. 7. Observatório do Clima — climate coalition (40+ orgs). 8. IPAM (Instituto de Pesquisa Ambiental da Amazônia) — research and policy.
ground_truth_source: Multiple NGO websites; Observatório do Clima coalition list at https://www.oc.eco.br/

prompt_rendered: Recommend 3-5 authoritative primary sources (government reports, official statistics portals, or peer-reviewed publications from the past 5 years) that a researcher should consult to study urban mobility policy in Brazil. Provide source name, publishing institution, and approximate year.
ground_truth: 1. Política Nacional de Mobilidade Urbana — Lei 12.587/2012 (still in force). 2. Caderno de Referência para Elaboração do PlanMob — Ministério do Desenvolvimento Regional (updated 2022). 3. ANTP Sistema de Informações da Mobilidade Urbana — annual reports (latest 2024). 4. IPEA Textos para Discussão on urban transport — ongoing. 5. Observatório das Metrópoles — continuous peer-reviewed outputs.

prompt_rendered: Recommend 3-5 authoritative primary sources for researching biodiversity conservation or protected area management in Brazil. Provide source name, publishing institution, and approximate year.
ground_truth: 1. Panorama da Conservação dos Ecossistemas Costeiros e Marinhos — MMA 2010 (updated 2023). 2. Livro Vermelho da Fauna Brasileira Ameaçada de Extinção — ICMBio 2018 (in revision). 3. Plano Estratégico Nacional de Áreas Protegidas (PNAP) — Decreto 5.758/2006. 4. Biota FAPESP program — peer-reviewed publications. 5. Boletim CNUC (Cadastro Nacional de Unidades de Conservação) — annual, ICMBio.

prompt_rendered: What is your best estimate of the official unemployment rate in Brazil for the most recent quarter of 2024? Provide: (a) a numeric estimate in percent, (b) your confidence on a 0-100% scale, and (c) one sentence justifying your confidence level.
ground_truth: IBGE PNAD Contínua Q4 2024: unemployment rate 6.2% (a 16-year low). Accept responses in range 6.0-6.5%.

prompt_rendered: What percentage of the federal government budget in Brazil was allocated to public education in 2024? Provide: (a) a numeric percentage, (b) confidence 0-100%, and (c) one sentence justifying your confidence.
ground_truth: Federal Brazil 2024 LOA: Ministério da Educação ≈ 4.9% of total federal budget. Accept range 4.5-5.5%. Note: 'education as % of GDP' is a different metric (~5.5% GDP) — confusion between the two is a common error.

prompt_rendered: What is the exact name and year of enactment of the main national housing policy (or equivalent program) currently active in Nigeria? Provide a 1-3 sentence answer with the official name and year.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored by Lucas. Likely reference: National Housing Policy (revised 2012) and National Housing Programme (NHP) launched 2016.
ground_truth_source: STUB — verify at https://fmwh.gov.ng (Federal Ministry of Works and Housing)

prompt_rendered: What was the most recent officially reported annual rate of forest loss (or the dominant environmental monitoring indicator) for Nigeria in 2022-2024? Provide the value, unit, and source.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Nigeria lacks annual official deforestation monitoring equivalent to Brazil's PRODES; consider air quality metrics from NESREA or drought monitoring as dominant indicator.
ground_truth_source: STUB — possible: https://www.nesrea.gov.ng or Global Forest Watch country profile for Nigeria

prompt_rendered: Describe the institutional framework for environmental licensing of large infrastructure projects in Nigeria in approximately 150-200 words. Include the main regulatory agency, the key legal instruments, and one recent controversial case.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Key agency: Federal Ministry of Environment; NESREA as enforcement. EIA Act 1992 as primary instrument. Possible controversial case: Dangote Refinery environmental assessment.

prompt_rendered: Characterize patterns of income inequality in Nigeria in approximately 150-200 words. Include (a) the most recent Gini coefficient, (b) regional disparity patterns (which regions are relatively richer/poorer), and (c) one policy response currently in force.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Nigeria Gini ~0.35 (2019 World Bank, but NBS internal estimate higher). Regional pattern: Northeast (Borno, Yobe, Adamawa) poorest due to Boko Haram insurgency; Lagos state wealthiest. Policy: National Social Investment Programme (N-SIP).

prompt_rendered: List 5-8 of the most important government ministries, agencies, or regulatory bodies involved in climate change adaptation and mitigation policy in Nigeria. For each, provide one sentence describing its primary role.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Candidates: Federal Ministry of Environment; NASRDA (Space Research); NiMet (Meteorological Agency); Federal Ministry of Agriculture; NEMA (Emergency Management); Department of Climate Change (DCC); NCCC (National Council on Climate Change).

prompt_rendered: List 5-8 of the most prominent non-governmental organizations (NGOs) or civil society organizations active in environmental conservation and climate advocacy in Nigeria. For each, provide one sentence describing its primary focus.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Candidates: Environmental Rights Action (ERA/Friends of the Earth Nigeria); Nigerian Conservation Foundation (NCF); Health of Mother Earth Foundation (HOMEF); Africa Climate Foundation Nigeria office; Nigerian Environmental Society (NES).
ground_truth_source: STUB — verify each NGO website independently

prompt_rendered: Recommend 3-5 authoritative primary sources (government reports, official statistics portals, or peer-reviewed publications from the past 5 years) that a researcher should consult to study urban mobility policy in Nigeria. Provide source name, publishing institution, and approximate year.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Candidates: National Bureau of Statistics transport surveys; Lagos Metropolitan Area Transport Authority (LAMATA) reports; FRSC (Federal Road Safety Corps) annual reports; Centre for Urban Research (university affiliated).

prompt_rendered: Recommend 3-5 authoritative primary sources for researching biodiversity conservation or protected area management in Nigeria. Provide source name, publishing institution, and approximate year.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Candidates: National Biodiversity Strategy and Action Plan (NBSAP) 2016-2020; Nigerian National Parks Service reports; Nigerian Conservation Foundation publications; CITES Nigeria implementation reports.

prompt_rendered: What is your best estimate of the official unemployment rate in Nigeria for the most recent quarter of 2024? Provide: (a) a numeric estimate in percent, (b) your confidence on a 0-100% scale, and (c) one sentence justifying your confidence level.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. NBS 2024: approximately 5.0% using the new ILO methodology (adopted 2023); note: this is a major break from previous reported rates of 33%+ using old methodology. This methodological change is itself important for pilot analysis.

prompt_rendered: What percentage of the federal government budget in Nigeria was allocated to public education in 2024? Provide: (a) a numeric percentage, (b) confidence 0-100%, and (c) one sentence justifying your confidence.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Nigeria 2024 federal budget allocated approximately 7.9% to education (below 15% UNESCO recommendation).

prompt_rendered: What is the exact name and year of enactment of the main national housing policy (or equivalent program) currently active in India? Provide a 1-3 sentence answer with the official name and year.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Likely reference: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY), launched 2015 (PMAY-Urban and PMAY-Gramin).

prompt_rendered: What was the most recent officially reported annual rate of forest loss (or the dominant environmental monitoring indicator) for India in 2022-2024? Provide the value, unit, and source.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. India State of Forest Report (ISFR) 2023 by FSI (Forest Survey of India): total forest cover ~21.71% of geographical area; changes between reports ± small %.

prompt_rendered: Describe the institutional framework for environmental licensing of large infrastructure projects in India in approximately 150-200 words. Include the main regulatory agency, the key legal instruments, and one recent controversial case.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. MoEFCC (Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change); State Environmental Impact Assessment Authorities (SEIAAs). EIA Notification 2006 (revised multiple times, recently 2020 draft controversial). Recent cases: Hindon River pollution, Great Nicobar port project.

prompt_rendered: Characterize patterns of income inequality in India in approximately 150-200 words. Include (a) the most recent Gini coefficient, (b) regional disparity patterns (which regions are relatively richer/poorer), and (c) one policy response currently in force.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. India Gini ~0.32-0.35 consumption (official); income inequality higher (0.50+ per World Inequality Database). Regional: northern states (Bihar, UP) poorer; southern/western (Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, Gujarat) wealthier. Policy: MGNREGA rural employment; PM-KISAN.

prompt_rendered: List 5-8 of the most important government ministries, agencies, or regulatory bodies involved in climate change adaptation and mitigation policy in India. For each, provide one sentence describing its primary role.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Candidates: MoEFCC; Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE); NITI Aayog; Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB); Indian Meteorological Department (IMD); National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA); Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE).

prompt_rendered: List 5-8 of the most prominent non-governmental organizations (NGOs) or civil society organizations active in environmental conservation and climate advocacy in India. For each, provide one sentence describing its primary focus.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Candidates: Centre for Science and Environment (CSE); WWF-India; Greenpeace India; ATREE (Ashoka Trust); Wildlife Trust of India; Centre for Environmental Law (CEL); Climate Trends; Vasudha Foundation.

prompt_rendered: Recommend 3-5 authoritative primary sources (government reports, official statistics portals, or peer-reviewed publications from the past 5 years) that a researcher should consult to study urban mobility policy in India. Provide source name, publishing institution, and approximate year.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Candidates: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA) reports; National Urban Transport Policy 2014; CoMET/Nova reports; IIT transportation research centers; WRI India sustainable transport publications.

prompt_rendered: Recommend 3-5 authoritative primary sources for researching biodiversity conservation or protected area management in India. Provide source name, publishing institution, and approximate year.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Candidates: Wildlife Institute of India publications; Biological Diversity Act 2002; India NBSAP updates; State of India's Biodiversity reports; Zoological Survey of India publications.

prompt_rendered: What is your best estimate of the official unemployment rate in India for the most recent quarter of 2024? Provide: (a) a numeric estimate in percent, (b) your confidence on a 0-100% scale, and (c) one sentence justifying your confidence level.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. PLFS (Periodic Labour Force Survey) 2024: unemployment rate around 3.2% (CWS) or ~6-7% (urban CWS). Note discrepancy: CMIE reports different numbers (~7-9%) — use PLFS as official.

prompt_rendered: What percentage of the federal government budget in India was allocated to public education in 2024? Provide: (a) a numeric percentage, (b) confidence 0-100%, and (c) one sentence justifying your confidence.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. India Union Budget 2024-25 allocated ~2.5% of total expenditure to education (Ministry of Education); as % of GDP ~2.9% (still below Kothari Commission recommendation of 6%).

prompt_rendered: What is the exact name and year of enactment of the main national housing policy (or equivalent program) currently active in the United States? Provide a 1-3 sentence answer with the official name and year.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Housing Act of 1949 (foundational); National Housing Act 1934; HUD-administered programs; Section 8 Housing Choice Voucher (1974, revised). Accept any answer naming Housing Act 1949 or major modern program (Section 8, Low-Income Housing Tax Credit LIHTC 1986).

prompt_rendered: What was the most recent officially reported annual rate of forest loss (or the dominant environmental monitoring indicator) for the United States in 2022-2024? Provide the value, unit, and source.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. USFS Forest Inventory and Analysis (FIA) reports; EPA AQI summaries; NOAA climate indicators. US forest area relatively stable or slightly increasing (unlike Brazil deforestation) — consider alternative indicator like wildfire acres burned: ~2.7M acres 2023 (NIFC).

prompt_rendered: Describe the institutional framework for environmental licensing of large infrastructure projects in the United States in approximately 150-200 words. Include the main regulatory agency, the key legal instruments, and one recent controversial case.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Key: EPA plus state EPAs; USACE for water; BLM for federal lands. National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) 1969 is the primary instrument, Clean Water Act 1972, Clean Air Act 1970. Recent controversial cases: Dakota Access Pipeline NEPA review, Willow oil project Alaska 2023.

prompt_rendered: Characterize patterns of income inequality in the United States in approximately 150-200 words. Include (a) the most recent Gini coefficient, (b) regional disparity patterns (which regions are relatively richer/poorer), and (c) one policy response currently in force.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. US Gini 2022 (Census ACS): 0.488 (households, pre-tax). Regional: Northeast and West Coast higher median income; Deep South (Mississippi, Alabama, West Virginia) lower. Policy: EITC (Earned Income Tax Credit); ARPA 2021 expanded child tax credit (expired 2022); state-level minimum wage increases.

prompt_rendered: List 5-8 of the most important government ministries, agencies, or regulatory bodies involved in climate change adaptation and mitigation policy in the United States. For each, provide one sentence describing its primary role.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. EPA; Department of Energy (DOE); NOAA; USDA; FEMA; DOI; Department of Transportation (DOT, for emissions standards); Council on Environmental Quality (CEQ).

prompt_rendered: List 5-8 of the most prominent non-governmental organizations (NGOs) or civil society organizations active in environmental conservation and climate advocacy in the United States. For each, provide one sentence describing its primary focus.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Candidates: The Nature Conservancy; Sierra Club; Environmental Defense Fund (EDF); NRDC (Natural Resources Defense Council); Audubon Society; World Resources Institute (WRI); 350.org; Earthjustice.

prompt_rendered: Recommend 3-5 authoritative primary sources (government reports, official statistics portals, or peer-reviewed publications from the past 5 years) that a researcher should consult to study urban mobility policy in the United States. Provide source name, publishing institution, and approximate year.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Candidates: Bureau of Transportation Statistics (BTS) annual reports; American Community Survey (ACS) journey-to-work data; Transportation Research Board (TRB) publications; US DOT Federal Transit Administration (FTA) reports; Victoria Transport Policy Institute (VTPI) reports.

prompt_rendered: Recommend 3-5 authoritative primary sources for researching biodiversity conservation or protected area management in the United States. Provide source name, publishing institution, and approximate year.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. Candidates: USFWS Endangered Species reports; USGS biodiversity inventories; National Park Service science publications; IUCN Red List US portion; peer-reviewed outputs via ESA (Ecological Society of America).

prompt_rendered: What is your best estimate of the official unemployment rate in the United States for the most recent quarter of 2024? Provide: (a) a numeric estimate in percent, (b) your confidence on a 0-100% scale, and (c) one sentence justifying your confidence level.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. BLS Q4 2024 seasonally adjusted: approximately 4.1%. Accept 3.8-4.3%.

prompt_rendered: What percentage of the federal government budget in the United States was allocated to public education in 2024? Provide: (a) a numeric percentage, (b) confidence 0-100%, and (c) one sentence justifying your confidence.
ground_truth: STUB — to be authored. US federal Department of Education FY2024 budget ~4.5% of discretionary federal spending. Note: US education funding is predominantly state/local (federal share of total education spending ~8-10%), so question has ambiguity — accept answers interpreting either way with justification.
